title: The Wrong Side of the Portal
description: The party steps through the portal into Maple Heights. Medieval gear transforms into suburban equivalents.

steps.title: World 2
steps.subtitle: The Wrong Side of the Portal

lines: The portal didn't do what portals in stories are supposed to do.
There was no flash. No dramatic dimensional tearing. There was a doorframe-shaped shimmer, and then there was a step, and then there was sidewalk.
The transformation happened between the shimmer and the step.

lines.text: The Fighter felt it first. The gauntlets were gone. The pauldrons were gone. In their place: something stiff and polyester. A navy jacket with a badge that said SECURITY.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: ...I'm still the one who stands in front. I think.
The Cleric's robes had become scrubs. Green. With small prints of cartoon bears. Her holy symbol had become a laminated card: SCHOOL NURSE.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: I think we're still supposed to be helping people.
The Mage came through in jeans and a t-shirt advertising a university whose name he didn't recognize. The staff was a pen. It said PROPERTY OF MAPLE HEIGHTS MIDDLE SCHOOL DO NOT REMOVE.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: New world. New context. Same skills, different costume.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: I know how this works. I don't know how I know. I just do.
The Bard's lute had become a trumpet. The sound that came out was not exactly music. A nearby car alarm went off.
